reasons: Diggie can reduce the value of Chip's portal-based crowd-control engage.
Chip wants to bring allies into a crowd-control pile, while Diggie can protect teammates during that moment.

proof.summary: Chip wants a portal engage into airborne or stun follow-up, while Diggie can use teamfight protection to reduce the crowd-control value of that engage.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Chip's real portal commit.
Diggie stays near the teammate Chip is trying to collapse on.
The team avoids entering Chip's portal fight before Diggie is ready.
failureCases: Chip baits Diggie's ultimate with a fake engage.
Diggie is caught before he can cast.
Chip's team delays the portal entry until Diggie's protection ends.

reasons: Valir can push away Chip or the first hero arriving through Chip's portal.
Chip's engage becomes weaker when the enemy team can immediately reset spacing.

proof.summary: Chip wants allies to arrive through a portal and collapse on one area, while Valir can disrupt the arrival path and reset the fight.
worksBestWhen: Valir watches the main portal location instead of chasing Chip.
Valir keeps knockback for portal arrivals.
Valir's team backs away from the portal focal point.
failureCases: Chip's team arrives from multiple angles.
Valir is the first target and gets crowd-control chained before casting.
Valir wastes knockback before the portal engage starts.

reasons: Chip's portal can group allies into a predictable area, which gives Atlas a punish window.
Atlas can counter the teamfight pattern Chip enables when multiple enemies arrive near the same portal point.

proof.summary: Chip wants to concentrate allies around a portal target, while Atlas can punish grouped arrivals if he holds his engage for the portal commit.
worksBestWhen: Atlas waits for multiple enemies to take the portal.
The fight happens near terrain or choke points.
Atlas has Flicker or a clean flank angle.
failureCases: Atlas engages before enough enemies arrive.
Chip's team spreads immediately after entering.

reasons: Kadita can punish portal arrivals because Chip's team often appears around a known main portal area.
Her burst can convert when enemies cluster too tightly after using Shortcut.

proof.summary: Chip creates a portal focus point, while Kadita can prepare burst around that point and punish grouped arrivals.
worksBestWhen: Kadita has vision of the main portal.
Chip's allies enter without checking Kadita's position.
Kadita waits for a high-value target to arrive.
failureCases: Kadita commits before the high-value target arrives.
Chip's team enters spread out or with crowd-control immunity support.

reasons: Franco can threaten Chip before Chip starts the portal play.
If Chip is hooked or suppressed before the setup, his team loses the clean engage trigger.

proof.summary: Chip needs time and positioning to create portal value, while Franco can punish him before or after Shortcut with pickoff pressure.
worksBestWhen: Franco controls river or bush vision.
Franco targets Chip before the portal has full team follow-up.
Franco's team can burst Chip quickly after suppression.
failureCases: Franco hooks Chip after Chip has already created a favorable portal collapse.
Franco's team cannot burst Chip through his durability.
Chip's team counter-engages through the portal while Franco is committed.
